title: Castela calcicola
intro: Castela calcicola, commonly known as the lime tree or "palo de tinte" in Spanish, is a small, deciduous shrub native to the coastal regions of Baja California, Mexico. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its numerous health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which have been shown to aid in the treatment of various ailments such as wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Castela calcicola include flavonoids, terpenoids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Castela calcicola is often prepared as a tea or a tincture, and is also used in topical applications to treat skin conditions and wounds.

In some regions, it is employed to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues. The plant is also believed to possess antimicrobial properties, which may help in fighting off infections. Further research is necessary to confirm the effectiveness and safety of Castela calcicola for medicinal purposes.
